title: phthirusa pycnophylla
intro: Phthirusa pycnophylla, also known as the 'cottony cup plant', is a species of epiphytic plant in the family Loranthaceae. It is a type of parasitic plant that grows on the branches of its host tree, obtaining nutrients through its roots. The medicinal properties of Phthirusa pycnophylla include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into infusions or decoctions, and are used to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
